policy_statement: Develop a comprehensive strategy to support islandable microgrids and district systems using renewable energy and behind-the-meter storage, especially at critical facilities, for grid emergencies (NYSERDA).
mechanism_description: NYSERDA plans to develop a strategy that supports islandable microgrids and district systems using renewable energy and behind-the-meter storage so critical facilities can maintain locally generated power during grid emergencies.
primary_causal_pathway: Climate hazard (storms/heat/wildfire) → grid outage risk → islandable microgrids + BTM storage → continued critical power supply → ↓ exposure/impacts
causal_mechanism_detail:
1) Change in human activity/system: Deployment and operational enablement of islandable microgrids/district energy systems and behind-the-meter storage at critical facilities (e.g., hospitals, shelters, water/wastewater, emergency services), including controls and interconnection that allow safe disconnection from the bulk grid.  
2) Mediating physical/engineering process: During grid disturbances, local generation plus storage provide stable voltage/frequency and ride-through capability while isolated (“island mode”), reducing dependence on compromised transmission/distribution assets.  
3) Climate-relevant outcome: Reduced disruption of essential services and reduced health/safety/economic damages during climate-driven extreme events that cause or exacerbate power outages (improved resilience and adaptive capacity).
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is maintaining essential electricity supply during extreme-weather-related grid outages by reducing exposure and sensitivity to power disruptions.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: Hazard-driven outages are the stressor; the policy enables islanding and storage so facilities retain power during emergencies, which directly reduces climate vulnerability and impacts.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: Although renewables and storage can reduce GHGs, the mechanism as defined (“grid outage resilience,” “critical facilities,” “emergencies”) makes service continuity the main climate-relevant effect, aligning with Adaptation rather than Mitigation or Resource Efficiency.  
3) Edge cases applied: If a microgrid program is primarily designed for emissions reductions/renewable integration with resilience as a co-benefit, it could lean Mitigation; however, the canonical mechanism explicitly centers outage resilience, so Adaptation remains primary for all policies in this mechanism class.  
4) Applicability to ALL instances: Any policy fitting “islandable microgrids and behind-the-meter storage support → grid outage resilience” shares the same dominant resilience pathway (maintaining power during outages), regardless of technology mix.  
5) Secondary subtype deferred: Whether specific implementations also deliver significant mitigation (e.g., diesel replacement, peak shaving) depends on project design and is deferred to policy-level evidence.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If implementations rely heavily on fossil backup generation (e.g., diesel) or are primarily intended for renewable integration/peak reduction rather than emergency islanding, mitigation/resource-efficiency considerations could become dominant; the provided mechanism description and wording (“grid emergencies,” “critical facilities,” “islandable”) supports Adaptation as primary.
key_indicators: “develop a comprehensive strategy”; “support development of islandable microgrids and district systems”; “behind-the-meter storage”; “especially in critical facilities”; “for use during grid emergencies”
co_benefits: Potential mitigation co-benefits from “renewable sources of energy” (reduced GHG/air pollutants) and possible energy cost savings, but not the primary stated objective.
instance_edge_case_notes: Could be implemented via incentives or infrastructure funding, but the stated instrument in the excerpt is the creation of a cross-agency “strategy,” so classified as planning-strategy rather than an incentive-program/infrastructure-investment.
